title: Canada GST/HST Calculator
name: Canada GST/HST Calculator
titleTag: Canada GST/HST Calculator | Smart Calculator Pro
metaDescription: Calculate GST, HST, and provincial sales tax by province in Canada. Estimate before-tax amounts, after-tax totals, and tax breakdowns with one tool.
intro: Estimate Canadian sales tax by province using a practical calculator that supports before-tax amounts, after-tax totals, and tax breakdowns.
shortDescription: Estimate GST, HST, and province-based sales tax totals in Canada with before-tax and after-tax workflows.
longDescription: This Canada GST/HST calculator helps users estimate sales tax totals using province-appropriate assumptions for GST, HST, PST, RST, and QST where relevant. It supports two common workflows: starting with a before-tax amount to find the after-tax total, or starting with a total after tax to work backward to the before-tax amount. It also breaks out the tax layers so the result is easier to review and explain.
problemSolved: Helps estimate province-appropriate Canadian sales tax totals and before-tax or after-tax amounts with a transparent breakdown.

inputs.label: Calculation Mode
inputs.explanation: Choose whether you are starting with a before-tax amount or an after-tax total.

inputs.label: Province or Territory
inputs.explanation: Select the Canadian province or territory so the calculator can apply the appropriate GST, HST, PST, RST, or QST assumptions.

inputs.label: Amount
inputs.explanation: Enter the amount that matches your selected workflow. Use a before-tax amount in the first workflow or a total-after-tax amount in the reverse workflow.

outputs.label: Before-Tax Amount
outputs.explanation: The amount before GST, HST, or provincial sales tax is applied.

outputs.label: Total After Tax
outputs.explanation: The full amount after all applicable sales tax assumptions are applied.

outputs.label: Total Tax Amount
outputs.explanation: The total combined sales tax in the result.

outputs.label: GST Amount
outputs.explanation: The GST portion when a separate GST layer applies.

outputs.label: HST Amount
outputs.explanation: The HST portion when the province uses a harmonized sales tax rate.

outputs.label: Provincial Tax Amount
outputs.explanation: The separate provincial sales tax layer where a province uses PST, RST, or QST instead of HST.

formula.label: Canada GST/HST Method
formula.expression: After-Tax Total = Before-Tax Amount x (1 + Combined Tax Rate); Before-Tax Amount = After-Tax Total / (1 + Combined Tax Rate)
formula.plainText: The calculator applies province-based sales tax rates to move from a before-tax amount to an after-tax total, or reverses that process when you start with the after-tax total.

formulaExplanation: When you start with a before-tax amount, the calculator applies the province's combined sales tax assumption to estimate the full after-tax total.
When you start with a total after tax, the calculator divides the total by one plus the combined rate to recover the before-tax amount.
For HST provinces, the calculator treats the sales tax as a single harmonized layer.
For provinces with separate GST and provincial sales tax layers, the calculator shows the GST portion and the separate provincial portion in the breakdown.
methodologyNotes: This calculator is a practical Canada sales tax estimator, not tax filing or accounting software.
Province and territory assumptions are based on current standard published GST, HST, PST, RST, and QST rates used for straightforward planning scenarios.
Some real transactions may be tax-exempt, zero-rated, or subject to special rules that this first version does not model.
Quebec is shown using a separate provincial tax layer for practical planning even though the provincial component is QST rather than PST.
This tool is best for estimate-based budgeting, invoice checks, and quick tax math.

example.scenario: A user in Ontario wants to estimate the total cost of a $100 purchase before tax.
example.resultSummary: The calculator shows a before-tax amount of $100, HST of about $13, total tax of about $13, and a total after tax of about $113.

resultInterpretation.defaultText: Use the breakdown as a quick planning estimate. The most important thing is that the province assumption matches the place where the tax rate applies.

thresholds.label: Total Tax Amount
thresholds.interpretation: A tax amount in this range is common for everyday purchases and invoice checks, but the exact layer depends on whether the province uses GST only, HST, or a GST-plus-provincial-tax structure.

warningMessages: Sales tax treatment can vary by province, product type, and transaction rules, so use this as an estimate unless you have confirmed the exact tax treatment.

faq.question: What is the difference between GST, HST, and PST in Canada?
faq.answer: GST is the federal goods and services tax. HST is a harmonized sales tax used in some provinces instead of separate federal and provincial sales taxes. PST is a separate provincial sales tax layer used in some provinces alongside GST.

faq.question: Why does the tax rate change by province?
faq.answer: Because provinces do not all use the same sales tax structure. Some use HST, some use GST plus a separate provincial tax, and some use only GST.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator backward from a total after tax?
faq.answer: Yes. Choose the reverse workflow and enter the after-tax total. The calculator will estimate the before-tax amount and tax breakdown using the province you selected.

faq.question: Why does Quebec use a separate provincial tax amount in this calculator?
faq.answer: Because Quebec uses GST plus QST rather than HST. This calculator shows the provincial layer separately for practical planning and breakdown clarity.

faq.question: Is this calculator enough for invoices or tax filing?
faq.answer: It is useful for quick planning and checks, but real invoices and tax reporting can depend on product rules, exemptions, and business registration details.

disclaimer: This Canada GST/HST calculator provides estimate-based sales tax results only. Actual tax treatment can vary by province, transaction type, exemptions, and business rules. Use it for planning, not tax filing or legal advice.
assumptions: Uses standard province-based GST, HST, PST, RST, and QST assumptions for straightforward before-tax and after-tax sales tax planning in Canada.
